label: About
title: About Pale Internet Herbarium
short_blurb: A daily generated front page where the image is the interface.
body: Daily Frontpage is a daily generated interactive front page. Each edition starts from recent saved signals and source research, becomes a new image-led scene, and stays explorable in the archive. The image is the interface: visible marks, objects, and surfaces in the scene open the real sources that shaped the day.

This iteration began with 108 recent saved-signal files and 76 captured links, then narrowed the page to 9 source windows around @jonaasw1: cool internet things pt. 11 - retro console games - type out classic books -... and @simicvm: Your travel itinerary, but as a tiny living 3D world. Actual geography, real-time..., plus 7 more. A source image from pbs.twimg.com helped set the palette, contrast, edge behavior, and gesture. The scene became Pale Internet Herbarium: Pale internet herbarium, diagrammatic fold, browserless media shrine: a severe warm-white field with a faint square grid and ghosted unreadable type at the far margins. Small source-bearing slips hover at varied yaw, casting hairline shadows; two restrained hero fragments anchor the orbit while pinhole apertures, bo... After the image was made, a plate-reading pass mapped 9 visible regions and the mask audit tightened them against upper-right folded triangular paper shard with green-black underlayer, lower-left torn layered media shard with blue, ochre, and red panes, and left and right faded marginal type columns.

typography.rationale: Bricolage Grotesque and Space Grotesk fit record, arcade, and listening-room editions while Fraunces keeps source captions tactile.
